actionUnits.condition: MonoBehaviour fileID 2592690533154406537 (Script GUID 1729fd01c90a64d5b8037e1eef0d000d) is Fire. This scene component matches the provided Fire script fields: isOn=0, timeToOnMin=40, timeToOnMax=60, maxNeighborDistance=4, onNeighborsThreshold=2, downMultiplier=2, minNeighborDownDistance=3, mainEffectGo=fileID 1849464941111748095. BoxCollider is present (no Rigidbody). There are no UnityEvent m_Calls on this object. Goal: validate auto-ignition path via neighbors. Preconditions required: neighbors list must contain at least 2 Fire instances within 4 units, with at least one neighbor located >=3 units below this object to set hasDownNeighbor=true (for faster countdown via downMultiplier). After neighbors are populated (via Fire.FindNeighbors externally or manually assigned), wait up to 60s for shouldTurnOn and timeToOn to elapse so this.isOn becomes true and UpdateFireState(true) plays particle system and notifies neighbors and FireController.Instance.FireChanged(+1).

With the same Fire MonoBehaviour (fileID 2592690533154406537), test extinguishing while fire is ON (from previous task or externally set). Simulate water hose hits that invoke Fire.WaterHit(Vector3) via the water system (IWaterInteractable). Apply hits at intervals < maxHitInterval (2s), e.g., ~1s, for total accumulated hit time >= totalHitTime (5s). Expect: isTurningOff true, currentHitInterval resets on each hit, currentHitTime accumulates and reaches threshold; then fire turns off, UpdateFireState(true) stops particle system and notifies neighbors and FireController.Instance.FireChanged(-1).

Recovery test for extinguish cancellation: ensure fire is ON, then apply a single water hit (invoking WaterHit via external water system), wait > maxHitInterval (e.g., 2.1s) without further hits. Expectation: isTurningOff becomes false and currentHitTime resets to 0 (extinguishing attempt aborted). No state change to OFF should occur.
